speaker: MACBETH
text: Hang out our banners on the outward walls.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The cry is still “They come!” Our castle’s strength

speaker: MACBETH
text: Will laugh a siege to scorn. Here let them lie

speaker: MACBETH
text: Till famine and the ague eat them up.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Were they not forced with those that should be ours,

speaker: MACBETH
text: We might have met them dareful, beard to beard,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And beat them backward home.

speaker: MACBETH
text: What is that noise?

speaker: SEYTON
text: It is the cry of women, my good lord.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I have almost forgot the taste of fears.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The time has been my senses would have cooled

speaker: MACBETH
text: To hear a night-shriek, and my fell of hair

speaker: MACBETH
text: Would at a dismal treatise rouse and stir

speaker: MACBETH
text: As life were in ’t. I have supped full with horrors.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Direness, familiar to my slaughterous thoughts,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Cannot once start me.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Wherefore was that cry?

speaker: SEYTON
text: The Queen, my lord, is dead.

speaker: MACBETH
text: She should have died hereafter.

speaker: MACBETH
text: There would have been a time for such a word.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Tomorrow and tomorrow and tomorrow

speaker: MACBETH
text: Creeps in this petty pace from day to day

speaker: MACBETH
text: To the last syllable of recorded time,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And all our yesterdays have lighted fools

speaker: MACBETH
text: The way to dusty death. Out, out, brief candle!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Life’s but a walking shadow, a poor player

speaker: MACBETH
text: That struts and frets his hour upon the stage

speaker: MACBETH
text: And then is heard no more. It is a tale

speaker: MACBETH
text: Told by an idiot, full of sound and fury,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Signifying nothing.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou com’st to use thy tongue: thy story quickly.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Gracious my lord,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I should report that which I say I saw,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: But know not how to do ’t.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Well, say, sir.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: As I did stand my watch upon the hill,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I looked toward Birnam, and anon methought

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Wood began to move.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Liar and slave!

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Let me endure your wrath if ’t be not so.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Within this three mile may you see it coming.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I say, a moving grove.

speaker: MACBETH
text: If thou speak’st false,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Upon the next tree shall thou hang alive

speaker: MACBETH
text: Till famine cling thee. If thy speech be sooth,

speaker: MACBETH
text: I care not if thou dost for me as much.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: I pull in resolution and begin

speaker: MACBETH
text: To doubt th’ equivocation of the fiend,

speaker: MACBETH
text: That lies like truth. “Fear not till Birnam Wood

speaker: MACBETH
text: Do come to Dunsinane,” and now a wood

speaker: MACBETH
text: Comes toward Dunsinane.—Arm, arm, and out!—

speaker: MACBETH
text: If this which he avouches does appear,

speaker: MACBETH
text: There is nor flying hence nor tarrying here.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I ’gin to be aweary of the sun

speaker: MACBETH
text: And wish th’ estate o’ th’ world were now undone.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ring the alarum bell!—Blow wind, come wrack,

speaker: MACBETH
text: At least we’ll die with harness on our back.
